title: lupinus angustifolius subsp. leucospermus
intro: Lupinus angustifolius subsp. leucospermus, also known as narrow-leaved lupin or lupin, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids, saponins, and other compounds. The health benefits of lupin include the potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and even provide antioxidant properties. Lupin can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
